Thunderstorms developed across southwest Idaho early Tuesday evening and produced heavy rain across portions of the Lower Treasure Valley, including the city of Boise. Social media, weather spotter, and emergency manager reports indicated flooding of many streets in downtown Boise and the North-End Boise area. Water overwhelmed stormwater drainages and flowed onto sidewalks and lawns. Water up to two feet deep left several vehicles stranded in the downtown area. The Connector, a major commuter route to downtown Boise was flooded, as were some apartments, and several buildings on the Boise State University campus. Rainfall rates over one inch an hour occurred with localized rainfall totals up to 1.85 inches between 6 PM and 10 PM MDT.
Numerous reports of tree damage, large branches down, via Social Media.
Numerous trees down. Social media reports showing a tree on a house and a tree down on power lines. Numerous power outages.

Flash flood produced debris, mudflow over the U.S. Highway 20. Mud/debris up to 3 feet thick reported over the highway. Highway closed for a couple of days by ODOT to clear debris. Some old uninhabited historic houses also flooded in the area. MRMS estimates 2-4 inches of rain over the basin upstream.
Large tree down.
From PIREP, small tornado to the surface, haze is making it hard to see. Likely a brief landspout.

Corrects previous hail report from 8 E Fairfield. Largest hailstones reported were estimated quarter-sized, half-inch hail preceded. Reported under core of storm.
Accumulating water on roadways. US20 traffic slowed to 40mph. Motorcyclists trapped. 2.65 inches of rain measured in the last hour.
Spotter reported thunderstorm wind gusts resulting in 3 large pine trees knocked down, small branches breaking, and one large branch breaking.
